overview: This Legacy midrange archetype leverages efficient black creatures alongside white and blue utility to control the early board while scaling into a resilient late-game presence. By utilizing cards like Nethergoyf and Dauthi Voidwalker, the deck applies immediate pressure while disrupting opponent mana and resources. Moonshadow provides a flexible finisher that can swing combat or clear opposing threats. The strategy balances aggressive early plays with defensive resource management to outvalue opponents over time.
win_conditions: Trading up into a favorable board state with efficient black creatures
Using Moonshadow's combat trick to secure lethal damage or remove key blockers
Outlasting opponents through resource denial and card advantage from midrange staples

keep_criteria: Hands with Nethergoyf or Dauthi Voidwalker paired with early removal or mana acceleration
Two-card hands containing Moonshadow and a cheap creature for turn-one pressure
Balanced hands with at least two lands and a mix of creatures and interaction
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (Swamps or duals), plus at least one early interaction (Thoughtseize, Fatal Push) or a 1B creature (Moonshadow/Dauthi Voidwalker).
Keep hands with Nethergoyf if supported by a land and a discard outlet.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or board presence
Mana-heavy hands with no creatures or interaction
Hands that cannot establish a turn-one or turn-two threat
Mulligan pure curve decks, hands with 0-1 lands, or hands with only expensive threats and no early board presence.

early_game: Play Nethergoyf on turn one to establish board presence and disrupt opponent's land drops
Use Dauthi Voidwalker to apply pressure while forcing opponent to spend resources on removal
Prioritize land drops and early interaction to stabilize against aggressive decks
Turn 1: Play a Swamp, cast Thoughtseize on a key non-basic land or early threat, or drop a 1B creature if holding lands.
Turn 2: Develop board with a second land, cast Nethergoyf if opponent has sacrificed creatures, or play a cheap deathtouch creature.
Prioritize establishing a 2-drop board presence over rushing
Nethergoyf scales best when opponents are forced to sacrifice to your removal or their own effects.
mid_game: Trade efficiently with opponent's threats while preserving Moonshadow for key moments
Leverage card advantage by playing around expected removal and board wipes
Transition to a more controlling posture by holding up black mana for interaction
Mana should be kept flexible for 1B spells and 2-drop creatures
avoid flooding with Swamps if you have multiple 1B threats.
Use discard to disrupt opponent's mana or key combo pieces.
Manage graveyard carefully—Nethergoyf benefits from opponent sacrifices, but you must avoid filling your own graveyard with unplayable cards if facing graveyard hate.
Commit threats when you have removal ready to answer their interaction or when Nethergoyf is already large.
Hold back if facing sweepers or if your hand is light on answers.
late_game: Use Moonshadow to swing combat, remove problematic blockers, or secure game-ending damage
Maintain board presence with resilient creatures while cycling through resources
Apply consistent pressure until opponent runs out of answers or mana
Nethergoyf is the primary win condition, especially when it reaches 5+ toughness.
Moonshadow and Dauthi Voidwalker provide deathtouch pressure that forces trades.
Use discard to strip their removal, then swing with Nethergoyf and deathtouch creatures.
Go all-in when you have established board control, your hand is light on threats but heavy on Nethergoyf/Moonshadow, and you've removed or bypassed their main interaction.
Commit when Nethergoyf is large enough to trade through their best removal or when you can close the game in 1-2 turns.

key_cards.name: Nethergoyf
key_cards.role: Early board presence and mana disruption
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to force opponent to answer immediately or fall behind; use its land-destruction ability to slow down ramp or control strategies.

key_cards.name: Dauthi Voidwalker
key_cards.notes: Deploy to apply pressure while forcing opponent to spend cards on removal; its enter-the-battlefield effect can disrupt graveyard-dependent strategies.

key_cards.name: Moonshadow
key_cards.notes: Hold up for combat to swing attacks, remove key blockers, or deal direct damage; use selectively to maximize tempo advantage and secure lethal.

sequencing_tips: Hold up black mana for interaction before committing creatures to combat
Play Nethergoyf before opponent's land drops to maximize disruption value
Save Moonshadow for moments when it can swing the game state or secure lethal damage
Common mistakes include overcommitting to the board too early, which leaves you vulnerable to sweepers, or failing to use discard effectively to disrupt opponent's key pieces.
Remember that Nethergoyf's power is tied to opponent sacrifices, so your removal and their own effects are your best friends.
Avoid filling your graveyard unnecessarily if facing graveyard hate.
Play around common sideboard cards like Rest in Peace or Surgical Extraction by keeping threats in hand.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early board presence with Nethergoyf and Dauthi Voidwalker while holding up removal for their key threats.
Apply steady pressure with efficient creatures and use Moonshadow to bypass counterspells or remove problematic permanents.
Disrupt their setup with early interaction and use Moonshadow to clear key combo pieces or finish the game before they assemble.

Work around it with discard to remove counterspells before they can cast them. Use cheap, efficient threats that can be cast in response to their spells. If they counter your removal, use discard to strip their hand and apply pressure with Nethergoyf, which is hard to counter if cast at instant speed or if they're out of mana.

Prioritize early disruption (Thoughtseize, Fatal Push) to hit their lands or key ramp spells. Use discard to remove their mana rocks or finishers. Nethergoyf scales well against ramp if they sacrifice creatures to your removal or their own effects.

Win by out-resourcing with better card advantage (discard, lifelink, or graveyard synergies). Prioritize trading efficiently with deathtouch creatures and use Nethergoyf as a late-game wincon. Sideboard in additional removal and discard to control the midgame. Track their hand and graveyard to avoid falling for their traps.

common_threats: Watch for opponent's discard targets (prioritize keeping Nethergoyf and removal).
Track their graveyard size and sacrifice outlets.
Note if they're playing around your removal with unblockable or hexproof threats.
Watch for mana development—if they're ramping, prioritize early disruption.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype: Mono Black Midrange, Format: legacy, Colors: W/U/B, Signature cards: Moonshadow, Nethergoyf, Dauthi Voidwalker
